arguments.task: Open and read official Slack first-party pages that support: (1) one-line product description from Slack official site, (2) Slack API auth methods from Slack developer docs, including every documented method and no others, (3) Slack pricing or plan/access page for self-serve vs gated access, (4) Slack Web API/Events/Socket Mode/SCIM or GraphQL evidence to determine API surface type and breadth, (5) any official Slack page about MCP/server availability. Return a concise structured summary with the exact canonical URLs actually visited and the supporting text for each field. Prefer pages that load via plain GET and avoid search snippets. If a page is JS-rendered or thin, note that explicitly.

raw_result.description: Slack is a work operating system and messaging platform for teams to communicate and automate work.
raw_result.auth_notes: Slack developer docs show OAuth 2.0 for apps ('Implementing OAuth') and multiple token-based auth types used with the Web API, including bot tokens and user tokens via bearer authorization. No first-party evidence gathered here for Basic auth or API keys.
raw_result.gating_reason: Slack offers a free plan and self-serve paid plans on its pricing page; Enterprise is additionally contact-sales gated, but a first toolkit can be built on self-serve access.

api_surface.breadth: Slack provides a broad REST-style API surface including Web API methods, Events, Socket Mode, and admin-oriented APIs such as SCIM and Audit Logs; no evidence gathered for GraphQL.
api_surface.mcp_notes: An official Slack help page for 'Model Context Protocol for Slack' was opened during browsing, indicating an official MCP-related offering/page exists, though the exact implementation details were not fully extracted.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Slack Web API methods are known to have method-specific rate limits, but no exact first-party rate-limit page text was extracted in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs observed for authentication, methods reference, pricing, and an official Slack MCP help page. Pricing evidence indicates strong self-serve availability for initial toolkit development.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The browser session gathered but did not fully extract supporting text for some structured fields, especially mcp_exists and the exact product description wording; these conclusions rely on partial browser evidence from opened official pages rather than fully quoted page text.
